title: With Sanofi and Pfizer Deals, Novavax Bets on 'Amplification Strategy' to Drive Vaccines Engine
text: Novavax has transitioned its business model from a struggling pandemic-era vaccine manufacturer to a partnership-driven platform company. With Sanofi and Pfizer deals in place, Novavax is pursuing an amplification strategy where partners commercialize and develop vaccine candidates using Novavax's proprietary Matrix-M adjuvant technology and manufacturing expertise. The Sanofi agreement represents a transformative partnership in which Sanofi took over commercial responsibility for Nuvaxovid COVID-19 vaccine starting with the 2025-2026 season, while Novavax earns milestone payments and royalties. The Pfizer partnership, announced in January 2026, gives Pfizer access to Matrix-M technology for use in up to two disease areas with potential milestone payments reaching $500 million and ongoing royalties. This dual partnership approach allows Novavax to leverage its proprietary technology platform across multiple partners rather than bearing the full commercialization burden itself. The amplification strategy significantly reduces Novavax's operating expenses while generating high-margin licensing and milestone revenues. The company has successfully pivoted from being a capital-intensive manufacturer competing directly with Moderna and Pfizer to becoming a technology licensing platform provider.
